title: Your Follower Count Stopped Driving Your LinkedIn Reach. The Data Proves It
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-03, Reading time: 4 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.thestateofbrand.com%2Fnews%2Ffollower-count-stopped-driving-linkedin-reach%3Futm_source=tldrmarketing/1/0100019e8d2a39d3-5e841716-738b-4d72-a534-dce2eca5b2ad-000000/I2LG83GwDZhcKPOZ1t6GtFZ4TuMRkyz0v8bZVeI_9nc=452
summary: Your Follower Count Stopped Driving Your LinkedIn Reach. The Data Proves It (4 minute read) Follower count no longer predicts post performance as LinkedIn's 360Brew algorithm prioritizes relevance, early engagement, and creator authority. Smaller, focused accounts now outperform larger ones, while reach is concentrating among niche creators. Followers still seed initial distribution, but strong early signals and clear topical focus now determine how far a post spreads.

title: 3 signal rule for lifecycle re-engagement
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-03, Reading time: 2 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.linkedin.com%2Fposts%2Fjacalynbeales_lifecyclemarketing-lifecyclemarketingtips-share-7467570524869697536-Lh4v%2F%3Futm_source=tldrmarketing/1/0100019e8d2a39d3-5e841716-738b-4d72-a534-dce2eca5b2ad-000000/_rOYij1GJsn90pVyVBVP8Jlznh66YOYcYp0afxFKEXI=452
summary: 3 signal rule for lifecycle re-engagement (2 minute read) The 3 signal rule improves re-engagement by targeting users who showed value, then lapsed, and hit friction. Instead of generic reminders, it focuses on high-intent users who are more likely to return. Define a clear “aha” moment, identify a common drop-off or error point, and trigger a short sequence that helps them complete that action. The goal is to guide users back to value, not just drive a login.

title: AI's brave new world of technical debt

summary: React Native Runtimes (Website) React Native Runtimes allows for executing components and functions across multiple JavaScript threads in React Native to prevent the main UI from lagging during heavy tasks. Performance is improved by offloading complex operations like chat screens, large lists, and background logic to dedicated secondary runtimes.

summary: Which popular beliefs about GenAI and software engineering hold up to research? To achieve meaningful gains, engineering leaders must shift their focus from individual coding assistance to systematic development process redesigns, such as streamlining code reviews.

summary: ERP vendors have lost control of the narrative. What's the story? (4 minute read) ERP vendors have lost the plot. summary: What is modern EDR? The upgrade to enterprise-grade endpoint protection (3 minute read) Modern EDR (endpoint detection and response) upgrades traditional antivirus by adding continuous monitoring, AI-driven behavioral detection, and automated response to catch and contain modern attacks like ransomware and fileless malware. It focuses on turning noisy alerts into clear, contextual incidents so even SMEs and MSPs can get enterprise-grade protection without needing large security teams.

comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-04, Reading time: 7 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fblog.zeptonow.com%2Fyour-cart-has-a-story-heres-how-we-learned-to-read-it-10ba9188f534%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019e921bc4fc-864192f6-b5fb-4632-ace5-5ec3b0821b7d-000000/vWJZz99TXQ4QNdFziNQMC0owLJ0h1kC0B3T8SEtdF1E=452
summary: Your Cart Has a Story. Here's How We Learned to Read It (7 minute read) Zepto built a Cart Contextual Model that treats shopping carts as “sentences” and uses a Transformer-based masked language model (MLM) to infer user intent in real time as items are added. By training on historical cart patterns with temporal, geographical, and product signals plus inverse-frequency masking to handle long-tail items, the model predicts what else the user will likely buy.

title: Debunking 8 data layout myths: why Liquid Clustering outperforms partitioning
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-04, Reading time: 11 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.databricks.com%2Fblog%2Fdebunking-8-data-layout-myths-why-liquid-clustering-outperforms-partitioning%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019e921bc4fc-864192f6-b5fb-4632-ace5-5ec3b0821b7d-000000/LfGHyJagT0iyYgOf1-b4B4Dm4Vdep9gHG9ySYLwEQj8=452
summary: Debunking 8 data layout myths: why Liquid Clustering outperforms partitioning (11 minute read) Databricks debunks 8 common myths about data layout, arguing that Liquid Clustering is superior to traditional Hive-style partitioning for modern lakehouses. Unlike rigid partitioning, Liquid Clustering dynamically organizes data using clustering keys that evolve over time, supports row-level concurrency, metadata-only operations, and works seamlessly across open table formats.

title: dbt Core v2 is here: still open source, now rebuilt for what's next
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-04, Reading time: 9 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fdocs.getdbt.com%2Fblog%2Fdbt-core-v2-is-here%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019e921bc4fc-864192f6-b5fb-4632-ace5-5ec3b0821b7d-000000/vMjD7Kn3hK2z2JhXk9npIiW6Aa5P_LtokDKhs9o0dhE=452
summary: dbt Core v2 is here: still open source, now rebuilt for what's next (9 minute read) dbt Core v2.0 alpha makes the Fusion engine's Rust-based runtime open source under Apache 2.0, unifying Core and Fusion around a shared foundation with faster parsing, Parquet artifacts, better local docs, simpler installs, and a tighter language spec. Fusion remains the recommended free CLI for most users, while Core v2 serves teams that need fully open source code or custom OSS builds.

summary: Diving deep into Redis's new array data type (25 minute read) Redis Array is a brand-new native data type (introduced in Redis 8.8) designed for constant-time positional access by index, filling a long-standing gap in Redis where position/index itself carries semantic meaning. It efficiently supports both dense and extremely sparse arrays using a hierarchical group-based structure, allowing fast random access, range queries, ring-buffer semantics, pattern matching across sparse data, and fixe...

title: Routing Multiple Query Engines with Iceberg
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-04, Reading time: 18 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flakeops.dev%2Fblog%2Frouting-multiple-query-engines-with-iceberg%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019e921bc4fc-864192f6-b5fb-4632-ace5-5ec3b0821b7d-000000/r6IN3PgWSgJatAxCXc2AtiX0WMTbyjVghKllfHRtegI=452
summary: Routing Multiple Query Engines with Iceberg (18 minute read) QueryFlux is an open-source Rust-based SQL routing proxy that intelligently directs queries across multiple query engines (Trino, Spark, DuckDB, Snowflake, Athena, Flink, etc.), sharing the same Iceberg tables. It handles protocol translation, dialect conversion via SQLGlot, cost-aware routing, concurrency control, and health-based failover.

title: Microsoft brings coreutils to Windows
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-04, Reading time: 2 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.osnews.com%2Fstory%2F145206%2Fmicrosoft-brings-coreutils-to-windows%2F%3Futm_source=tldrnewsletter/1/0100019e924e3cb8-771fd421-2ad5-458b-b5de-08076cb71cd0-000000/prHpQfG2ZvyZb9YLgXzdqaYm_BG8YuXqujvhZm4VDgM=452
summary: Microsoft brings coreutils to Windows (2 minute read) coreutils for Windows brings Unix-style command-line utilities to Windows. It provides developers with the everyday tools they already use on other platforms to script, automate, and process text. There are some differences from other versions of the library, such as the use of Windows path separators. Several commands that aren't useful on Windows are excluded. The binary can be installed through WinGet.

summary: sandboxed (GitHub Repo) sandboxed is an open source engine for AI app-builder products. title: Every byte matters
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-04, Reading time: 6 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Ffzakaria.com%2F2026%2F06%2F01%2Fevery-byte-matters%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019e92531bad-9a3d70cc-67b1-4489-94fb-d555dee86030-000000/kLu3MHrdZZzwcfzOXxXjYO4p23CNMZGKJBHvumMv8nA=452
summary: Every byte matters (6 minute read) Software performance is deeply affected by how data interacts with hardware components like memory tiers and cache lines, going beyond traditional algorithmic complexity. By using a "Struct of Arrays" instead of an "Array of Structs" to tightly pack data, devs can optimize sequential processing and achieve up to thirty times performance improvement by minimizing the size of data structures.

summary: Running an AI-native engineering org (8 minute read) The Claude Code team eliminated outdated processes by adopting just-in-time planning and AI-assisted coding, which required restructuring roadmaps and roles. summary: Ideogram 4 (GitHub Repo) Ideogram 4 is an open-weight text-to-image model. It was trained from scratch and not a fine-tune of any existing model. title: Exploring vs Exploiting: The Two Modes of Product Discovery
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-05, Reading time: 9 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.antmurphy.me%2Fnewsletter%2Fexploring-vs-exploiting-the-two-modes-of-product-discovery%3Futm_source=tldrproduct/1/0100019e974062ec-56214e5f-837e-4df6-8625-3437bf207f5e-000000/ySKG6psiphgiLfYcI647xieF-fgmW3TbnaiJpshk238=452
summary: Exploring vs Exploiting: The Two Modes of Product Discovery (9 minute read) Great product teams balance two modes of discovery: exploring new opportunities and improving known ones. Most teams default to measurable optimization, but lasting innovation comes from making room for open-ended learning, observation, dogfooding, AI research, and continuous feedback.

title: The Retrieval Layer between Your Data and Your AI Outputs is a Product Decision
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-05, Reading time: 8 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fmoderndata101.substack.com%2Fp%2Fthe-retrieval-layer%3Futm_source=tldrproduct/1/0100019e974062ec-56214e5f-837e-4df6-8625-3437bf207f5e-000000/FFmoYOpRiQz7obbZiFXeCRXpT63_5JCJ1quYya2yTAw=452
summary: The Retrieval Layer between Your Data and Your AI Outputs is a Product Decision (8 minute read) The “retrieval layer” in RAG systems is the most consequential product decision because model outputs are bounded by what retrieval returns. Retrieval is broken into three phases: query shaping, find/filter, and assembling context for the model - which emphasizes validating retrieval to prevent silent, incomplete failures.

title: AI is not a line item
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-05, Reading time: 8 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Ffrontierai.substack.com%2Fp%2Fai-is-not-a-line-item%3Futm_source=tldrproduct/1/0100019e974062ec-56214e5f-837e-4df6-8625-3437bf207f5e-000000/GOLfM0OYnlNGNJJ5FeY4918jjsq49kHqeM-zZz2RoBk=452
summary: AI is not a line item (8 minute read) Enterprises are mismanaging AI spend by treating it as a single budget line item and using token usage as a proxy for productivity. Budgeting and constraints should be tool- or use-case-specific because different AI agents create different business impact and ROI.

title: Mission vs Goal: A PM's Guide to Driving Real Impact
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-05, Reading time: 14 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.aakashg.com%2Fmission-vs-goal%2F%3Futm_source=tldrproduct/1/0100019e974062ec-56214e5f-837e-4df6-8625-3437bf207f5e-000000/-NicFoQ8Pu7ZphYSTxuCESj2WqAjn3g9tPbQwigBMHs=452

title: Building Software Is Learning

summary: Building Software Is Learning (6 minute read) Building new software is inherently a learning process because you cannot fully specify your requirements before reality pushes back. title: Open Code Review
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-05, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fgithub.com%2Falibaba%2Fopen-code-review%3Futm_source=tldrnewsletter/1/0100019e974f3f7b-f4d4a933-9aa1-468d-987b-1e034f4e223d-000000/wD_xaq0z1JzqnUjskJO5T5Xh8VBOZfe7OH2TPIYo7qE=452
summary: Open Code Review (GitHub Repo) Open Code Review is an AI-powered code review CLI tool that reads git gives, sends challenged files to a configurable LLM via an agent with tool-use capabilities, and generates structured review comments with line-level precision. It can read full file contents, search code bases, inspect other files for context, and produce deep reviews. Open Code Review's core philosophy is to combine deterministic engineering with an agent, letting each handle what it does best.

summary: Keeping Code Reviews From Dragging (11 minute read) AI has made PRs cheaper to generate, but review capacity has not scaled with them, making slow reviews more costly through context switching, stale branches, and shallow follow-up reviews. title: Talking to JSI in Swift: what changed in SDK 56
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-05, Reading time: 15 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fexpo.dev%2Fblog%2Ftalking-to-jsi-in-swift%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019e97798877-e37e60b9-060b-4ac6-8bcb-ce037b053e73-000000/DbxHstNXRWQrogoo5sWupxmW4y6GHKiEsF3K5c8doO0=452
summary: Talking to JSI in Swift: what changed in SDK 56 (15 minute read) Expo SDK 56 has an architectural rewrite for native modules on Apple platforms by allowing Swift to communicate directly with JSI. This change removes the previous Objective-C++ middleware, resulting in performance gains between 1.6 and 2.3 times faster than older versions. By using Swift 5.9's C++ interoperability and non-copyable types, the new system has strict memory safety while simplifying the internal development stack.
